title: Foster Home Placement - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Foster Home Placement 57 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS AID FOSTER CARE AND ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program Information Coordinator (PIC) provides information to families interested in foster care or adoption. Staff guides families through the certification and home study process for foster care and adoption programs. Foster parents receive ongoing training to maintain certification and support. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Foster parents must be age 21 or older and complete necessary training and application Hours: Family support: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Information sessions: -Infant adoption: 1st Tuesday of most months -Foster care adoption: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 222-9661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Native youth and families involved with foster care by offering cultural support, connecting them to community resources, and assisting with reunification or finding permanent homes. Services include group meetings, support classes, and family events. Also recruits and supports Native American foster families, guiding them through the certification process. Services for foster parents include Positive Indian Parenting classes, Sibling and Family Night events, and help with genealogy and regalia. Does not provide legal advice. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American youth ages 0-24 who are in state or tribal foster care systems, their birth parents, and their foster parents. Young adults who have aged out of foster care can receive services through age 24. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides therapeutic support to children, teens, and their families. Services include counseling, therapeutic day treatment schooling programs, and in-home supportive services. Offers therapeutic Foster Care for forster children and teens who have emotional, behavioral, and/or medical needs. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Youth involved in the child
